acceptance_criteria: A factor matrix exists with mention/model/omit decisions and rationale.
The matrix identifies where each factor belongs in the blog.
The design states clearly why some factors need only scoped mention rather than full modeling.
The outcome can be handed off cleanly to both planner/editorial and implementer/code lanes.
constraints: Do not let scope expansion bury the main thesis.
Prefer explicit limitation language over weak simulation claims.
Keep the distinction between issuer, currency user, and quasi-sovereign central.
description: Build a factor matrix for bonds/securities, maturity structure, rollover risk, holder composition, collateral/liquidity plumbing, treasury-central-bank operations, external constraint, and distribution. For each factor, decide whether the blog needs a substantive paragraph, a short limitation note, a toy model, or no new treatment. The objective is to make the post complete enough to avoid sounding biased or overfitted without turning it into a full monetary plumbing textbook.
goal: Decide which omitted factors must be explicitly integrated into the blog, which merit toy-model treatment, and which should remain stated limitations.
implementation_notes: Completed the omitted-factor scoping pass. Created a durable design brief at .ai/handoffs/money-minsky-policy-mismatch-factor-refinement.md with factor triage, blog placements, effort guidance, prioritized tasks 025-030, and Claude-ready pseudocode for the most valuable computational extensions. Recorded ADR-0007 to keep the refinement scoped as a completeness layer rather than a full bond-market rewrite.
title: Scope omitted financial-plumbing factors for money-minsky-policy-mismatch
